frame_analyses.description: Trainee preparing instruments and loading the suture.
Suture is being placed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
Suture being placed through the second mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alignment appears slightly off from the mark.

frame_analyses.description: Beginning the first throw with a surgeon's knot using the right hand.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Maintains tension and forms double throw correctly.

frame_analyses.description: Completing the second throw with a hand switch to left hand.
Performing the third throw with another hand switch.

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut of suture ends observed.
The slit in the drain is fully closed.

knot_assessments.notes: Secure and properly executed surgeon's knot.
Second throw executed correctly with hand switch.
Third throw completed with appropriate hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit fully closed with no visible gap.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated consistent technique with effective hand switching and knot tying. Suture placement was precise, though slightly off-mark.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on minimizing suture placement deviation from marks.
Maintain consistency in tension during throws.
strengths: Effective closing of the Penrose slit with secure knots.
